findings.title: Minting trusts the requested ERC20 deposit amount instead of the amount actually received

findings.title: The proxy silently accepts low-gas ETH transfers and bypasses logic execution

best_agent_match.title: The proxy silently accepts low-gas ETH transfers and leaves the ETH stuck

rejected_candidates.title: ETH mints create unbacked shares on non-WETH loan pools
rejected_candidates.reason: In this source, `mint` is not payable. The ETH-handling branch inside `_mintToken` is therefore unreachable through the normal proxy/implementation dispatch, so the reported exploit path does not execute.

rejected_candidates.title: Arbitrary Call in updateSettings Allows Fund Theft
rejected_candidates.reason: `updateSettings` is an owner or designated lower-admin control surface for upgrades/configuration. That is a privileged trust assumption, not a permissionless vulnerability in the protocol logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Malicious Target Contract Can Drain Proxy Funds
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-controlled upgradeability is the intended proxy design. A malicious owner or compromised governance key is a trust-model risk, not a code flaw specific to this contract.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Borrow Allows Arbitrary External Calls
rejected_candidates.reason: Executing an arbitrary callback is the normal flash-loan design. The function is `nonReentrant` and enforces post-call balance restoration before returning.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Critical Addresses Create Single Points of Failure
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a centralization and maintainability concern, not a concrete exploitable vulnerability demonstrated by the code.

rejected_candidates.title: LowerAdmin Role Has Full Control Without Timelock
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/trust-model observation about privileged administration rather than a protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Return Value Check on Token Transfer in burn
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `burn` uses `_safeTransfer`, which checks low-level call success and decodes the returned boolean when present.

rejected_candidates.title: Oracle Price Feed Dependency Without Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic oracle dependency alone is not a reportable finding here. No contract-specific validation bypass or exploit path was substantiated from this code.

rejected_candidates.title: Floating Pragma Solidity Version
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. The source uses a fixed pragma `solidity 0.5.17`, not a floating pragma.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Event Emissions for Sensitive Operations
rejected_candidates.reason: Operational visibility issue only; it does not by itself create realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: IERC20 Interface Missing Return Value for transfer/transferFrom
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a reportable vulnerability in this implementation. The transfer helpers already tolerate optional return data and revert on explicit false returns.
